headline: Lost Alpine Flower Rediscovered in Indian Himalayas After 158 Years
meta_description: Researchers confirm the presence of Cyananthus hookeri, a rare purple-blue alpine flower, in northeastern India—158 years after its last documented sighting.
summary_short: A distinctive alpine herb with striking purple-blue flowers has been rediscovered in India's remote Eastern Himalayas, marking the first confirmed sighting in 158 years.
body:
In the high-altitude meadows of northeastern India, botanists have confirmed the presence of Cyananthus hookeri, a small alpine plant with delicate purple-blue flowers that last graced scientific records in 1867. The rediscovery, made in Chuna Valley in Arunachal Pradesh's Tawang district at an altitude of 3,600 meters, represents far more than a single botanical find—it reveals how much remains unknown in Earth's remote ecosystems. "What impressed us most was that such a distinctive species had remained undetected despite extensive botanical exploration in the eastern Himalaya," said Sudhanshu Sekhar Dash, a botanist with the Botanical Survey of India. "This highlights how remote alpine ecosystems still harbor poorly documented plant diversity."

The plant itself is a marvel of adaptation. Growing in mat-like clumps with densely hairy stems, C. hookeri has evolved extraordinary defenses against mountain harshness—its robust, hairy cushions shield it from severe weather and extreme conditions. Yet this resilience exists within precarious constraints. The research team's fieldwork revealed fewer than 50 mature individuals in the wild, confined to rugged alpine terrain with unpredictable weather and an extremely narrow flowering window.

This vulnerability prompted the researchers to recommend that C. hookeri be classified as nationally endangered in India. The path forward requires not discovery alone, but commitment: long-term population monitoring, deeper ecological understanding, and community-led conservation efforts. In a time of accelerating environmental change, the reappearance of this purple-blue flower serves as both a reminder of what persists in hidden places and a call to protect it.
quick_facts: First confirmed sighting in India since 1867—a gap of 158 years
Found at 3,600 meters in Chuna Valley, Arunachal Pradesh
Fewer than 50 mature individuals documented in the wild
Distinctive purple-blue flowers with hairy throats
